Writing: Grade 1
Pre-A1
Unit Themes
Hello World!
My Classroom
My Body
Farm Animals
My Family
Food & Fun
Grade 1
Pre-A1
Text Types (Writing)

Name card: write my name & draw myself
Label: colour the letters of my name

Label a classroom picture (5–6 words)
Copy 2 simple sentences with support

Label body parts on a drawing (6 words)
Copy: I have two eyes and one nose

Label farm animals (5 words)
Copy & draw: My favourite animal is a ___

Fill-in family tree labels
Copy: This is my ___ . He/She is ___

Draw & label: My plate (3–4 foods)
Copy: I like ___ . I don't like ___
Grade 1
Pre-A1
Idea Development & Process

Draw myself → teacher scribes name
Trace letters of my name

Look at picture → circle object → copy word
Use word bank for labelling

Draw a body → teacher guides labelling
Trace and copy body-part words

Draw a farm → copy animal names from board
Sequence picture cards: story retell

Draw my family → use sentence frame
Match family word to picture before writing

Draw my plate → select food words from bank
Complete sentence frame with a food word
Grade 1
Pre-A1
Language Conventions (Writing)

Verb TO BE: I am, You are, He/She is
Subject pronouns: I, you, he, she, it
Article: a / an (introduction)
▸ Bloom: Remember

TO BE questions: Is this a…? Yes/No
Demonstrative: this / that
Singular nouns with articles
▸ Bloom: Remember–Understand

TO BE: I have (body parts)
Adjectives: big, small, long, short
Basic adjective + noun order
▸ Bloom: Understand

Can (positive): The cow can moo
Plural nouns (regular): cats, dogs
Conjunction: and
▸ Bloom: Understand

Possessive adjectives: my, your, his, her
TO BE: She is my grandma
Numbers as adjectives: two sisters
▸ Bloom: Understand–Apply

Like + noun: I like apples
Don't like + noun: I don't like milk
Full simple sentence: subject + verb + object
▸ Bloom: Apply
Grade 1
Pre-A1
Writing Reflection

Sticker chart: I can say my name ✓
Draw how I feel today (happy/sad face)
Graduate Profile: Communicator

Thumbs up/down: Did I learn new words?
Draw my favourite classroom object
Graduate Profile: Inquirer

Self-assess with smiley face scale
Share one new word I learned today
Graduate Profile: Reflective

Draw my favourite animal + copy its name
Share with a partner: my animal
Graduate Profile: Caring

Draw my family & share with the class
Exit ticket: say one sentence about family
Graduate Profile: Open-minded

End-of-year portfolio: my 6 drawings
Oral sharing: my favourite unit
Graduate Profile: Balanced
Grade 2
Pre-A1→A1
Unit Themes
All About Me
My Home & Family
Animals & Pets
My Town
Food & Health
Hobbies & Play
Grade 2
Pre-A1→A1
Text Types (Writing)

Self-description card: 30–50 words
Draw and label: what I look like

House description: 40–60 words
Label furniture in a room drawing

Animal description: 50–70 words
It has… It can… It eats…
Simple postcard from a place in town: 50–70 words

Healthy eating poster: 5 bullet points
Sentences: I should / I shouldn't eat…

Hobby paragraph: 60–80 words
My favourite hobby is… I like it because…
Grade 2
Pre-A1→A1
Idea Development & Process

Draw myself → fill sentence frames
Copy corrected draft from board

Room drawing → label → write 3 sentences
Word bank + sentence frames

Animal fact web → draft 4 sentences
Peer share: is everything clear?

Simple outline: Where? What to do there?
Draft postcard using sentence frames

Brainstorm healthy/unhealthy foods (two columns)
Draft 5 sentences with should/shouldn't

Mind map: my hobby → draft paragraph
Self-check: Does it make sense?
Grade 2
Pre-A1→A1
Language Conventions (Writing)

TO BE: all forms (am, is, are, was, were – intro)
Have/has got: I have got blue eyes
Adjectives of appearance
▸ Bloom: Remember–Understand

Prepositions of place: in, on, under, next to
There is / There are (introduction)
Singular/plural nouns – regular
▸ Bloom: Understand

Can / can't: ability
Present Simple 3rd person: It lives, It eats
